unit_title: Function Call Stack & Recursion | Reading Material
content:
# Function Call Stack & Recursion   
### Stack  
  
Stack is a data structure that stores items in an Last-In/First-Out manner.  
  
  
![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/qFuu9RG5FQS-axcMgHpVR0fk1hZQmP4ctuYSl8ebo2iCdJaogFJdgDg5bw2es2cOsNQDvqFxdJSemSg_-hi-O7iMxZqoZDvZZ4in5Ch6gs1BEHLqrIdhmT103j0_ylqGgppBTex59Kw)  
  
  
## Calling a Function  
  
Calling `function_1()` inside `function_2()`  
  
![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/1e89c597-74a5-4b6d-a6a2-bc8dc14fca8e.png)  
  
#### Code  
  
```python  
def get_largest_sqr(list_x):  
    len_list = len(list_x)  
    for i in range(len_list):  
        x = list_x[i]
        list_x[i] = x * x
    largest = max(list_x)  
    return largest  

list_a = [1,-3,2]  
result = get_largest_sqr(list_a)  
print(result)
```  
  
#### Output  
  
```  
  
9  
```

In the above code calling functions are `len()` and `max()` inside `get_largest_sqr()`

### Sum of Squares of List Items

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/d2c90913-dad6-4d42-8a92-c9669a288c29.gif)

#### Code
```python
def get_sqrd_val(x): 
    return (x * x) 
  
def get_sum_of_sqrs(list_a): 
    sqrs_sum = 0
    for i in list_a: 
        sqrs_sum += get_sqrd_val(i) 
    return sqrs_sum

list_a = [1, 2, 3] 
sum_of_sqrs = get_sum_of_sqrs(list_a) 
print(sum_of_sqrs)
```

#### Output

```

14
```

# Function Call Stack

Function Call Stack keeps track of function calls in progress

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/1017946f-81e5-4b48-97a6-aba165eb0221.png)

## Recursion

A function calling itself is called a **Recursion**
![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/b328e2fc-a6fe-496b-aee0-b64b86f3e377.png)

Let's understand recursion with a simple example of multiplying N numbers

### Multiply N Numbers

```python
def factorial(n):  # Recursive Function
   if n == 1:  # Base Case
       return 1
   return n * factorial(n - 1)  # Recursion
num = int(input())
result = factorial(num)
print(result)
```

**Base Case**

A recursive function terminates when base condition is met

#### Input

```

3
```

#### Output

```

6
```

### Without Base case

#### Code

```python
def factorial(n):
    return n * factorial(n - 1)
num = int(input())
result = factorial(num)
print(result)
```

#### Input

```

3
```

#### Output

```

RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded
```
